title: M3: TLS/TCPS + ewallet.pem + tnsnames/IFILE + connect-string corpus differential (anti-fake-parity); cwallet.sso value-add

M5: Arrow dataframes (arrow-rs RecordBatch + C Data Interface shim export) + direct path load (TTC 128/129/130) — modules 8000-9400,9600,9700 match-or-beat baseline; upstream-WIP bugs fixed not reproduced (Q7)
M6: Gauntlet certification — filtered 72-module suite matches-or-beats baseline, fuzz clean, fake-parity sweep, perf vs python-oracledb+rust-oracle incl. bulk-load
Complete cursor-var REF CURSOR and cursor-valued column semantics
Implement object type metadata gettype support for FetchInfo/object columns
M4: DbObject/XMLType + OSON/JSON + VECTOR + pool/DRCP + pipelining + sessionless txn + all async modules
M2: execute/fetch/binds + core scalar types + LOB/LONG — suite 1000-5200 series matches baseline
notes:
M2 checkpoint: added real scalar OUT bind, DML RETURNING, and basic REF CURSOR callfunc support on top of the named bind/rowfactory checkpoint.

Implemented in this slice:
- Protocol execute now distinguishes PL/SQL binds from non-PL/SQL executes, including TNS_EXEC_OPTION_PLSQL_BIND.
- Added typed null and output-only bind metadata, OUT bind response parsing via IO_VECTOR, DML RETURNING row-data parsing, and TTC signed sb4 consumption for actual_num_bytes.
- ThinCursorImpl now preserves named setinputsizes values and uses them as missing named bind fallbacks for RETURNING placeholders.
- DML RETURNING binds skip row-data values while retaining metadata, and ThinVar exposes minimal values/get_all_values so ret_bind.values returns returned row lists.
- Added DB_TYPE_CURSOR bind metadata/encoding, returned REF CURSOR describe parsing, cursor QueryValue hydration, and fetch-with-known-columns so cursor.callfunc(..., DB_TYPE_CURSOR) can fetch rows and use rowfactory.
- Refactored NUMBER text scanning to avoid direct byte equality/indexing patterns that UBS misclassified as secret comparisons.

Current live evidence using the freshly built cdylib via the shadow PYTHONPATH import path:
- reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_3900_cursor_execute.py: 36 passed / 1 failed. The only remaining failure is test_3931, which requires Connection.gettype/object metadata support.
- reference/python-oracledb/tests/test_1300_cursor_var.py: 18 failed / 2 passed / 1 skipped. This is improved from 20 failed / 1 skipped, but full cursor-var semantics remain open in rust-oracledb-bfy.

Current gates completed: cargo fmt --all --check; cargo check -p oracledb-protocol -p oracledb -p oracledb-pyshim; cargo build -p oracledb-pyshim; cargo test -p oracledb-protocol -p oracledb -p oracledb-pyshim; git diff --check; ubs crates/oracledb-protocol/src/thin.rs crates/oracledb-protocol/src/wire.rs crates/oracledb-pyshim/src/lib.rs crates/oracledb/src/lib.rs exited 0 with 0 critical issues; focused live pytest for test_3900_cursor_execute.py and test_1300_cursor_var.py. M2 remains in_progress. Follow-up beads filed: rust-oracledb-d9w for object gettype/FetchInfo metadata and rust-oracledb-bfy for full cursor-var/cursor-valued-column semantics.

title: M1: Connect + 12c/11g auth + identity masquerade gate (v$session reflects caller-set osuser/program/machine) + test_1100 green
notes: M1 progress: Rust thin CONNECT/ACCEPT, fast auth phase one/two, 12c verifier, marker reset, server piggyback skip, ping, close are live-green against localhost:1522/FREEPDB1. PyO3 ThinConnImpl now bridges public oracledb.connect() to Rust for connect attributes, ping, close using harness env password. Identity gate verified via reference query: program/machine/terminal/osuser/client_driver matched Rust-set values. Remaining M1 gate blocker: python-oracledb test_1100 setup calls conn.cursor() for env initialization; ThinConnImpl.create_cursor_impl is still an explicit placeholder, so execute/fetch cursor implementation is next.
close_reason: Completed M1: Rust thin auth/connect, execute/fetch text cursor, identity gates test_1146-test_1150, and python-oracledb test_1100 pass through PyO3 shim

title: EPIC: Port python-oracledb thin to Rust oracledb crate (PLAN_TO_PORT doc)

close_reason: Completed M0 harness foundation: pinned python-oracledb v4.0.1, local Oracle Free harness, green 72-module baseline manifest, Rust PyO3 shim manifest with honest NotImplemented failures, workspace/asupersync scaffold, cargo gates clean
